response.justification: Bogdan brings exceptional geospatial domain expertise through active GISCollective development and proven ability to design large-scale distributed systems handling millions of requests per second. However, his last production C++ work was in 2014-2015, with recent years concentrated on TypeScript/Node.js web development rather than systems-level work. The role requires modern C++ (or Rust) as a primary skill, and he demonstrates neither current nor recent experience in either. Additionally, his geospatial background focuses on data collection and mapping platforms, not navigation routing systems or pipeline optimization. An interview could assess his capacity to return to systems programming and navigate routing-specific concepts, but the technical ramp-up risk is substantial.
strengths: Rare geospatial domain expertise with actively maintained GISCollective platform deployed at production scale, directly relevant to routing tile systems
Proven systems-level thinking from microservices infrastructure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests/second using async I/O and high-performance databases
Strong software craftsmanship foundation with emphasis on TDD, custom testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and rigorous quality practices across all roles
concerns: Last production C++ work was 2014-2015 at National Instruments; 10-year gap with no recent systems-level development, recent focus entirely on web/TypeScript
No demonstrated experience with navigation routing systems, real-time traffic data pipelines, routing tile generation, or data freshness optimization
No Rust experience listed as alternative to C++, and no indication of working with mission-critical reliability/latency requirements at infrastructure scale
